title: elymus philadelphicus var. pendulus
intro: Elymus philadelphicus var. pendulus, also known as drooping wheat grass, is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as indigestion and diarrhea, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Elymus philadelphicus var. pendulus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and roots in hot water or a solvent, and can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
